name: Taibilla River Trail
name_original: Pedro Andrés desde Nerpio — circular por el valle del río Taibilla
description: This moderate 13.1-mile touring cycling route from Nerpio takes you through the scenic Taibilla river valley, passing ancient walnut trees a
region_intro: Touring cycling around Nerpio offers routes through mountainous terrain and river valleys in the south of Albacete province. The region is characterized by varying elevations, including the Sierra de las Cabras, and the Taibilla river which provides flatter sections. Cyclists can navigate routes that feature scenic overlooks and diverse natural environments. The landscape combines challenging climbs with more relaxed paths along watercourses. Best touring cycling routes around Nerpio The most popular touring cycling route is Taibilla River Trail, a 13.1 miles (21.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the picturesque Taibilla river. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Nerpio - Taibilla Reservoir Loop, a moderate 16.0 miles (25.8 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Mirador de la Borriqueta — circular desde Nerpio, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Nerpio is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and scenic overlooks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate river paths to challenging mountain ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nerpio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Nerpio?
A: Nerpio offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find routes through mountainous terrain, including the Sierra de las Cabras with challenging climbs, as well as more relaxed paths along the picturesque Taibilla river valleys. The varying elevations provide a mix of strenuous ascents and winding descents, ensuring a varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes suitable for less experienced riders?
A: Yes, while many routes in Nerpio are challenging, there are options for moderate touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363683900/taibilla-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taibilla River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.1-mile (21.2 km) route that follows the scenic river. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363754492/nerpio-taibilla-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerpio - Taibilla Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.0 miles (25.8 km) and offers views of the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Nerpio is rich in natural beauty. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic vistas from points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despiernacaballos Pass&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dramatic scenery. You might also encounter ancient trees like the almost 500-year-old &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plantón del Covacho&lt;/a&gt; walnut tree, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Toba Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2091573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning views.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging touring cycling routes in Nerpio?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363772000/mirador-de-la-borriqueta-circular-desde-nerpio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Borriqueta — circular desde Nerpio&lt;/a&gt; cover 53.4 miles (85.9 km) with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363783585/taibilla-reservoir-and-river-valley-loop-from-nerpio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taibilla Reservoir and River Valley Loop from Nerpio&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 48 miles (78 km) and featuring substantial climbs.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit while cycling in Nerpio?
A: You can incorporate cultural stops into your rides. The Taibilla Castle, an 11th to 15th-century Muslim fortress, offers historical interest and views of the Taibilla valley. Nerpio is also home to significant cave art, with sites like Solana de las Covachas providing a unique glimpse into prehistoric human settlement. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5897202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerpio&lt;/a&gt; itself is a charming place to explore.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Nerpio are designed as loops. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363772000/mirador-de-la-borriqueta-circular-desde-nerpio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Borriqueta — circular desde Nerpio&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive tour of mountainous terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363754492/nerpio-taibilla-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerpio - Taibilla Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Nerpio?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, Nerpio's mountainous climate suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for touring cycling. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures, making the climbs more enjoyable and the overall experience more comfortable than the heat of summer or the chill of winter.

Q: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the routes?
A: Yes, you can find places to refuel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noguera del Molino&lt;/a&gt;, an old mill converted into a restaurant, offers typical dishes of the Sierra del Segura region. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5897202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerpio&lt;/a&gt; itself provides a pleasant atmosphere with local amenities. You might also find facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hospedería Río Zumeta&lt;/a&gt; which can offer refreshments.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Nerpio area?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Nerpio area, offering a variety of experiences from moderate river trails to challenging mountain loops. More than 100 touring cyclists have already explored these routes using komoot.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Nerpio?
A: Touring cyclists often praise Nerpio for its combination of varied and scenic landscapes. The region offers everything from challenging mountain climbs with breathtaking views, such as those found in the Sierra de las Cabras, to more serene rides along the Taibilla river. The rich natural environment and opportunities to discover historical sites and charming villages also contribute to a highly enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Nerpio?
A: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5897202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerpio&lt;/a&gt; and other villages in the area are generally accessible by car. You can typically find parking within or near these settlements, providing convenient starting points for your touring cycling adventures.
tips:
Nerpio is a Spanish municipality located southeast of the Iberian Peninsula, in the province of Albacete, within the autonomous community of Castilla-La Mancha. It is located at the southern end of the Sierra del Segura region. It limits with the Albacete municipalities of Yeste and Letur; with Huéscar and Puebla de Don Fadrique, in Granada; with Moratalla, in Murcia; and with Santiago-Pontones, in Jaén. In addition, its municipal area is the southernmost in the province of Albacete and in all of Castilla-La Mancha.

By road it is 156 km from Albacete, through Elche de la Sierra and Ayna, or 189 km, along the Venta del Olivo route, somewhat faster than the previous one, as it runs largely by highway. It is 134 km from the Murcian capital.

The Solana de las Covachas cave complex is a cultural asset belonging to the World Heritage Site. It is an open-air museum, where you can take a guided tour.
More information on the website: https://www.turismosierradelsegura.es/es/25-Experiencias/36-Visita-guiada-a-las-pinturas-rupestres-de-Solana-de-las-Covachas.htm

Old walnut about 500 years old, which a few years ago dried up. Preserving the trunk to be able to contemplate the dimensions that it came to reach. It is located on the left bank of the road that goes from Nerpio to Pedro Andrés.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Pedro Andrés from Nerpio — circular through the valley of the river Taibilla route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring bicycle route typically takes about 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete. This estimate is for cycling and may vary depending on your pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is considered moderate. It requires a good physical condition from cyclists, featuring an elevation gain of approximately 390 meters over its 21.2-kilometer length. While much of it is on paved paths, there may be some unpaved or challenging sections.

faqs.question: Where does the Pedro Andrés from Nerpio cycling route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts in Nerpio, a municipality in the province of Albacete. As a starting point within a settlement, parking should be readily available in or around the town center.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are often in autumn, when the centenarian walnut trees display vibrant ochre, brown, and golden hues, or in winter with the first snows, offering a different kind of serene beauty. Spring and early summer are also pleasant before the peak heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Sierra del Segura region?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails like this one within the Sierra del Segura region. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions like the Rock paintings of Solana de las Covachas, guided tours may have associated costs.

faqs.question: Is the Pedro Andrés from Nerpio cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is outdoors, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when cycling, to ensure their safety and the safety of others. Be mindful of local regulations regarding pets in natural areas and near historical sites.

faqs.question: What interesting sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable features. You'll start in the tranquil settlement of Nerpio, pass by the World Heritage-listed Rock paintings of Solana de las Covachas, and see the impressive Plantón del Covacho, an ancient walnut tree. You can also admire Taibilla Castle and enjoy views of the Taibilla Reservoir.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain offers a mix, with much of the route on paved paths suitable for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for some unpaved or more challenging sections. The route winds through the serene valley of the Taibilla River, surrounded by natural beauty and centenarian walnut trees.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other trails. You'll find it crosses small portions of the Circular Nerpio - Peña Jarota, Ruta Micológica Mingarnao, and Ruta del Zarzalar Enlace GR-68/GR-66.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the Taibilla valley cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Nerpio, where you can find amenities. Along the valley itself, the focus is more on natural immersion, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What makes the Taibilla River valley special for cyclists?
faqs.answer: The Taibilla River valley offers a unique blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. Cyclists can enjoy the tranquil flow of the river, the majestic centenarian walnut trees, and the opportunity to visit historical sites like the Rock paintings of Solana de las Covachas. It's an ideal route for those looking to combine sporting activity with exploration in a peaceful setting.

waypoints.name: Rock paintings of Solana de las Covachas
